122556.explanation: Referenda can increase resident involvement but are not suitable for every topic. Besides referenda, there are other good ways to let residents participate and have influence.

122559.explanation: We welcome these kinds of initiatives; they help farmers make their businesses future-proof. Naturally, within reasonable limits.

122560.explanation: Initiatives from residents can contribute to the energy transition, but support must be applied carefully and fairly. The municipality must weigh case by case what kind of support is appropriate and what role it has.

122561.explanation: New distribution centers place great pressure on space, landscape, and infrastructure. By being cautious here, we protect the character of the environment and choose a balanced and sustainable spatial development.

122562.explanation: We believe the arrival of an asylum seekers center must be carefully considered. Local capacity and facilities play an important role, and we must ensure any reception is feasible and well integrated.

122563.explanation: Employers know their seasonal workers’ needs best and can directly provide good and safe housing. This keeps responsibility with the companies and prevents the housing of our own residents from being jeopardized.

122566.explanation: We believe a municipal fireworks display is unnecessary. Residents should retain the space to organize New Year's Eve traditions like carbiding themselves.

122567.explanation: Free bulky waste collection incurs high costs for the municipality. We believe residents should contribute a small fee to keep the system fair and affordable for everyone.

122568.explanation: We believe noise standards at festivals and parties should be stricter. Events such as Rijnweek cause nuisance to many residents when the wind is unfavorable, and better rules help limit noise pollution.

122571.explanation: It's a nice idea to pay sports club memberships for people on welfare benefits, but it is questionable whether this really is our task.

122573.explanation: We believe every primary school child should have the chance to take music lessons. With municipal subsidies, music education becomes accessible to everyone, regardless of the family's financial situation.

122574.explanation: We believe sports clubs are themselves responsible for their financial health. We can support with advice or facilities, but structural financial contributions are not our task.

122578.explanation: We believe additional camera surveillance is unnecessary. It does not suit the small-scale and personal character of Rhenen, and current measures are sufficient to ensure safety.

122579.explanation: We believe a women's shelter in Rhenen is definitely a good step, if there is a need. It offers safe accommodation for victims of violence and helps them receive protection and support.

122583.explanation: We believe holiday homes should be allowed to be used as permanent residences. In times of high housing prices and strong demand, this helps make additional living space available for people who want to live here.

122585.explanation: We believe people on welfare benefits can do socially meaningful work. It offers meaningful daily activities and can also be a step towards paid work and greater involvement in society.

122587.explanation: The current policy to provide assistance with poverty is already generous. Increasing the income threshold may help, but we must carefully consider whether it is necessary and feasible within our resources.

122591.explanation: We believe that people who need care should always have the option to choose a healthcare provider that matches their faith, culture, or beliefs. This promotes appropriate care and respect for personal convictions.

122592.explanation: We believe that price is an important factor when selecting organizations for youth care, but it should not be the only criterion. Quality, effectiveness, and appropriate care must always come first, although price should definitely be considered carefully.

122593.explanation: We believe that the property tax (ozb) can be lowered. Homeowners already pay a lot of taxes, and a lower ozb would relieve the financial pressure on this group of residents.

122594.explanation: We believe that the dog tax should not be abolished. It contributes to covering the costs the municipality incurs in combating the nuisance caused by dog waste.

122597.explanation: Shopping can easily be done on other days, and maintaining rest days supports families, employees, and the small-scale character of Rhenen.

122599.explanation: We believe that solar panels should not be placed on agricultural land. They are better placed on roofs; agricultural land should primarily remain suitable for growing crops and preserving farming activities.

122601.explanation: We believe the establishment of a coffee shop should be prevented. Coffee shops can cause nuisance, drugs are addictive, and society bears the costs of addiction care.

122603.explanation: We believe that high residential towers are not appropriate in Rhenen. They do not fit the character of our municipality.

122605.explanation: There are currently sufficient parking spaces in Rhenen.

122607.explanation: The widening is going much too slowly; this must be accelerated quickly.

122608.explanation: A lot of money has already been spent on a new harbor. As far as we're concerned, that is enough.

122609.explanation: Wind turbines spoil the landscape and our residents are not in favor of them.

122610.explanation: An interchange without level crossings is still our ideal.

122556.explanation: As residents, we govern Rhenen together. When a subject is clearly relevant in Rhenen, a referendum can be a nice and fair way to make a decision. This way we involve people in local democracy.

122559.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to earn money with their farms. A store selling good products from local land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

122560.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity in the same place, so the grid is less burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

122561.explanation: In recent years, far too many huge distribution centers have been built in the Netherlands. They spoil the landscape and much of the work is done by poorly paid labor migrants. We want to stop more distribution centers from arriving. This also protects our small retailers.

122562.explanation: We must shelter refugees, not because The Hague demands it but because we are in solidarity with people who have to flee. We will work on a small-scale AZC location. We actively involve our residents both in the preparation and the implementation of the shelter location.

122563.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must also ensure that these people have decent housing that is not dependent. This must be done without causing inconvenience to our own residents. There is no place in our municipality for exploitation and nuisance.

122566.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one where everyone comes together, with one big fireworks show organized jointly by Achterberg, Elst, and Rhenen. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a cozy New Year's Eve together.

122567.explanation: Furniture, old mattresses, and other junk on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This reduces hassle and results in cleaner streets.

122568.explanation: We have few festivals and parties in Rhenen. The rules for these, for example for the Rijnweek, are strict enough.

122571.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy, satisfaction, social contact, and keep you healthy. But for people on welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should remove barriers for these people, especially children, so they can join sports clubs.

122573.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

122574.explanation: Sports provide a lot of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy and sports clubs are often also meeting places. If a sports club collapses, everyone suffers. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in when necessary.

122578.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

122579.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A women's shelter is indispensable for our municipality. We could also organize this together with Veenendaal, Wageningen, or Utrechtse Heuvelrug.

122583.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution to the housing shortage. But as long as the need is as high as it is now, some people can use them. It is important that they can register the holiday home as their permanent address as long as necessary so that people are not left to fend for themselves.

122585.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation and trust from the municipality. Additional demands and conditions cause uncertainty and create new problems, as the childcare benefits scandal clearly demonstrated.

122587.explanation: People with an income just above the welfare threshold often do not qualify for many schemes that make life just a bit easier. We therefore want people with an income up to 140% of the welfare standard to be eligible for this assistance.

122591.explanation: Care happens very close to home. It must feel safe and trustworthy. We are much further from home when people start avoiding care.

122592.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth help and youth care are enormous. You cannot solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest option. We must primarily ensure that youth care is available and accessible to those who need it.

122593.explanation: The municipality does not levy taxes without reason. These revenues are necessary to perform duties that directly affect the lives of Rhenen residents. The more efficiently we use these revenues, the less is needed. But just like an increase, a decrease is not an end in itself but a consequence.

122594.explanation: The dog tax is a relic from the Middle Ages (dog cart). Today the dog is mainly a companion animal. For example, for elderly singles, the cost of the dog tax must not be a barrier to getting a dog.

122597.explanation: This topic is not currently an issue in Rhenen. Shopping Sundays can sometimes be a problem for small shopkeepers because they also want a day off when their children are at home.

122599.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want to, they should be able to use their land for this. We do ask to combine solar panels with more biodiversity, for example by letting flowers and other low plants grow.

122601.explanation: Rhenen has one coffee shop. That is sufficient. It does not need to be removed, but there should be no additional ones.

122603.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and action is needed. With high-rise buildings, you can quickly add many homes. We say: build upwards in a suitable location, such as in Rhenen North. This way we make good use of our valuable land. Of course, it must fit the surroundings.

122605.explanation: This is mainly an issue in Rhenen. We must ensure enough parking spaces at the edge of Rhenen’s center, such as near Paardenveld and Buitenomme. We want to keep cars that don’t belong in the center out. This keeps Rhenen’s center safe and healthy.

122607.explanation: Twice a day, Rhenen is full of stationary traffic. The Rijnbrug is one of the weak points and must therefore be widened as soon as possible. But further along the N233 towards Veenendaal, measures must also be taken and around the intersection near the station, otherwise the traffic jams will remain!

122608.explanation: There are plans for a beautiful green harbor area. Funds have been set aside for this. We do not need to spend any more money on this now.

122609.explanation: We contribute to generating our own energy. Besides solar panels on roofs, for example through solar fields combining arable land and grassland with solar panels. For a good mix, we believe a wind turbine should not be missing either. After all, the sun does not always shine.

122610.explanation: We need more good (bicycle) connections from Rhenen towards Achterberg (and vice versa). But also to neighboring municipalities like Veenendaal, Ede, and Wageningen. A (bicycle) tunnel or bridge provides an important solution.

122561.explanation: We need space for our local entrepreneurs.

122562.explanation: Multiple small-scale shelters are also possible, provided the national government covers all costs.

122563.explanation: Often not feasible on private property. Responsible for decent shelter in general: yes.

122567.explanation: Many people cannot dispose of it themselves.

122571.explanation: Promotes a healthy lifestyle and connection with the community.

122578.explanation: Can increase safety in dangerous places. Also consider better and more lighting.

122579.explanation: Precisely outside the municipality. In the municipality where both partners live, the chance of meeting is too great.

122585.explanation: Solidarity must come from both sides.

122587.explanation: Current level is fine. Work must pay off.

122592.explanation: All care organizations are inspected for quality.

122593.explanation: The property tax rate is above the national average.

122594.explanation: Maintain at the current level, do not increase further.

122597.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves.

122599.explanation: First solar panels on roofs, then solar fields.

122603.explanation: It is allowed to add one or two floors. Often not allowed now.

122605.explanation: There is no space for it.

122607.explanation: First ensure that the North-South connection is not blocked for longer than 3 months during the construction of the bridge. Whether the widening will help is still in question. The bottleneck (the viaduct) remains.

122608.explanation: Not necessary. Stadshaven will be realized in 2026 thanks to the VVD and the CDA.

122610.explanation: There is budget and subsidy for it.

122556.explanation: The opinion of the residents is very important. We want to develop policy with the residents of Rhenen in an open and transparent atmosphere. Residents are informed early about plans and participate in development. We want to achieve this through closer cooperation with residents.

122559.explanation: Agriculture, nature, housing, and recreation in the rural area can coexist well, as long as there is understanding and consideration for each other. We consider a healthy agricultural sector important and also see opportunities for developing small-scale recreational facilities.

122560.explanation: Rhenens Belang supports all forms of sustainability that benefit our residents, companies, and organizations. We want to stimulate this through a neighborhood-focused approach and targeted information. We offer financial support through a loan with low or no interest.

122561.explanation: It must fit within the scale of the municipality. It must also provide local benefits, such as employment. The establishment of a possible distribution center is only possible if the infrastructure (such as roads) is adequate. Mobility and traffic development must be secured.

122562.explanation: As long as the municipality is bound by the dispersion law, a reception center (AZC) may only proceed if there is support. Local safety must be ensured. Decision-making must fit society, care, education, and must not result in extra costs for Rhenen.

122563.explanation: There is currently high demand and too little supply of affordable housing, both for purchase and rent, in our municipality. Housing seasonal workers on the regular market puts even more pressure on the housing market.

122566.explanation: We look at what is sensible, workable, and affordable for the residents of Rhenen, companies, and organizations. Rhenens Belang believes the municipality should support initiatives, not by subsidizing or organizing, but by facilitating.

122567.explanation: The costs for waste processing – and thus housing expenses – will rise in the coming years. By organizing the waste processing process more intelligently, these costs for residents can be limited. Local taxes should not increase because of this. Nor should it cause neglect.

122568.explanation: There are already good national regulations for noise standards at festivals. Festivals fall under recreation and tourism and are important drivers of the economy of the municipality of Rhenen. Of course, the diversity of our neighborhoods and residents must always be taken into account.

122571.explanation: Rhenens Belang believes no one should live in poverty. The municipality uses a Cunerapas with a choice budget, among other things, for sports. Paying for sports subscriptions goes too far; associations already contribute. The focus is on low-threshold customized support and cooperation. For youth there is the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture.

122573.explanation: This is already arranged in Rhenen. It can also be arranged through the Youth Fund Sport & Culture Rhenen.

122574.explanation: The municipality can support and facilitate them. Not in the form of subsidies or otherwise, but by adopting an active, cooperative, and open attitude. We aim to promote the self-reliance of sports clubs and create healthy, future-proof clubs.

122578.explanation: Camera surveillance only where it truly increases safety and the chance of catching offenders, without violating privacy or increasing burdens. Effective and affordable deployment is paramount.

122579.explanation: Every resident must feel safe, comfortable, and welcome in our municipality. If a shelter for victims of domestic violence is needed for this, Rhenens Belang fully supports it. The establishment of such a shelter must be developed in consultation with the region.

122583.explanation: The infrastructure - such as neighborhood access and road network - must be in order. For permanent residence in holiday homes, Rhenen housing seekers are given priority.

122585.explanation: Rhenens Belang values appreciation and support and encourages people on social assistance benefits to voluntarily contribute. Volunteers are indispensable to our society. This way, we strengthen society together.

122587.explanation: RB focuses on fair income distribution: poverty arrangements must be aligned at the national level. Increasing the municipal poverty threshold is done in consultation with other municipalities to prevent arbitrariness and disadvantages for low incomes. Measures may not increase local taxes.

122591.explanation: Quality care is paramount. Care based on identity is possible, but if it leads to higher costs or scarcity, it must not disadvantage general care. Rhenens Belang is there for all residents, regardless of background, age, religion, or orientation. Everyone counts.

122592.explanation: There is a correlation between the care provided, results achieved, and costs. Early detection and a broader tailored approach are important.

122593.explanation: Local taxes should remain unchanged where possible. Rhenens Belang wants to limit and, if possible, reduce housing costs by leveraging new developments, cost reduction, and realistic planning. Residents are central to Rhenens Belang.

122594.explanation: Dog poop nuisance is the number one annoyance in Rhenen. Currently, dog tax revenues are not spent on dog-related matters. As long as the dog tax is not abolished, the rate should be based on the actual costs of dog policy (purpose tax).

122597.explanation: Rhenens Belang is for all residents, young and old, religious or not, without distinction. Everyone must feel safe and welcome. For entrepreneurs, we proactively think along about more opening days; entrepreneurship is encouraged. It must fit within the diverse character of Rhenen.

122599.explanation: Rhenens Belang does not want solar fields. Roofs of homes and businesses still offer sufficient potential for generating sustainable solar energy in our municipality. We want to promote solar energy by initiating collective purchasing and installation.

122601.explanation: Every resident must feel safe, comfortable, and especially welcome in our municipality. The establishment of a coffeeshop may only proceed if there is public support. We also find it important that the safety of the living environment is guaranteed, including the social safety of residents and entrepreneurs.

122603.explanation: High-rise buildings only in places where they do not damage the rural character of Rhenen. We build according to need. High-rise and adding floors to existing buildings can provide affordable housing, which is much needed. Moreover, Rhenen has much nature and therefore beautiful vistas.

122605.explanation: Rhenens Belang wants to support and facilitate entrepreneurs. Not in the form of subsidies or otherwise, but by adopting an active and open attitude towards entrepreneurs. So if entrepreneurs indicate a need for parking spaces, we are open to cooperation.

122607.explanation: By 2030, after widening, the Rhine Bridge will still be insufficient for increasing traffic and will not solve the infrastructure problem. A second river crossing is the best alternative, both in terms of traffic and finances, improves the N255 connection, and protects livability. Governing means thinking ahead.

122608.explanation: We support a small-scale transient or marina for economic and recreational boost. Traffic, parking, and enforcement must be taken into account during planning to prevent nuisance afterwards.

122609.explanation: No wind turbines inside or outside our municipal boundaries. Rhenens Belang believes that the side effects - on the environment and health - of wind turbines are too great. They are neither sustainable, cost-effective, nor suitable in a circular economy.

122610.explanation: The N233/Achterbergsestraatweg intersection must be grade-separated, so local and provincial traffic are separated. This improves the livability of Rhenen and Achterberg. The province pays; Rhenen only contributes to local connections.

122556.explanation: D66 wants to seriously involve residents in major decisions. For significant issues, a non-binding referendum can help gather opinions and enrich debate, while the municipal council retains ultimate responsibility for decision-making.

122559.explanation: Entrepreneurs provide employment and contribute to a healthy local economy. They deserve room to develop and realize their ideas. A municipality that thinks along and supports makes entrepreneurship attractive and future-proof.

122560.explanation: D66 wants to support small-scale energy initiatives, such as local energy hubs and sharing locally generated energy.

122561.explanation: We need more space in the industrial area to relocate businesses currently in town centers or for expanding existing businesses. Large distribution centers do not fit there; there is no space and they cause unwanted nuisance.

122562.explanation: We take responsibility for a fair share in the reception of refugees and status holders. For newcomers, whether status holders or from the Randstad, we aim to enable them to participate as much as possible from the outset and quickly build a network in Rhenen.

122563.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for adequate housing of labor migrants, including decent living space, wages, and care conditions.

122566.explanation: Organizing a joint fireworks display can be part of a safe, festive New Year's Eve. This must have a clear added value, given the costs involved.

122567.explanation: There are currently sufficient options to bring bulky waste yourself to the waste station. Free bulky waste collection increases costs.

122568.explanation: We actively encourage local events, which must comply with current noise standards.

122571.explanation: Everyone counts! Access to a sports club or culture must not depend on your wallet. People with low incomes must receive financial support for membership of a sports club so everyone can participate.

122573.explanation: Not for everyone, only for those who need extra support. We believe access to music lessons should not depend on your wallet. There must be financial support for people with low incomes so everyone can participate.

122574.explanation: Sports clubs have an important role in meeting and connecting people. We believe there should be subsidies to support club life.

122578.explanation: Urban development must explicitly consider the position, experience, and safety of girls and women. This can be done, among other ways, by mapping locations perceived as unsafe and proposing improvements.

122579.explanation: There is provision available in the region. For residents who need it, this is accessible.

122583.explanation: D66 wants to tackle housing shortages but also protect the recreational function. Therefore, permanent residence is not appropriate. Long-stay can be a temporary solution under strict conditions, provided it has no undesirable effects on surroundings and parks.

122585.explanation: We choose customization instead of a mandatory counter-performance. With a Participation Budget, we offer residents simplicity, trust, and autonomy, focused on what they need to get back to work, while the municipality works more efficiently.

122587.explanation: More and more people with low or middle incomes struggle to make ends meet. By raising the income limit, the municipality prevents people from falling just outside arrangements and provides timely help to prevent debts.

122591.explanation: D66 believes care must be accessible to everyone, regardless of gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, or background, and differences must be taken into account in policy and training. This means good inclusive care, without individual preferences causing shortages or unequal access.

122592.explanation: In youth care, quality comes first; children and young people deserve good support. Price is a factor but must never overshadow care quality. D66 chooses expert, effective help with responsible use of resources.

122593.explanation: We focus on affordable living by keeping property tax manageable with existing resources. Housing costs remain appropriate for services like waste, sewage, roads, libraries, and poverty schemes, without unnecessarily burdening residents.

122594.explanation: The dog tax helps the municipality with enforcement and keeping public spaces clean. This way, it remains clean, safe, and pleasant for everyone. Of course, dog owners must clean up their own dog poop.

122597.explanation: D66 wants to offer shopkeepers and residents more flexibility. Additional shopping Sundays stimulate local entrepreneurship, make the center more attractive to visitors, and provide more shopping opportunities without harming the living environment.

122599.explanation: D66 wants to promote sustainable energy, and agricultural land can contribute to the energy transition. We prefer solar panels on roofs and residual lands.

122601.explanation: D66 advocates regulated and safe cannabis sales. A ban makes the trade invisible and unsafe. Establishments are allowed, but not in the immediate vicinity of schools to protect young people and ensure proper supervision.

122603.explanation: Due to the severe housing shortage, building upwards is necessary to create sufficient homes. D66 chooses flats of 4-5 floors where urban-planning-wise possible, to use space efficiently while preserving the village character. Towering high flats are not desired.

122605.explanation: There is already sufficient parking capacity in and just outside the center. D66 wants to focus on an attractive, livable center and safe cycling routes so visitors can come comfortably without creating additional parking pressure in the core.

122607.explanation: Broadening the Rhine bridge is essential for better traffic flow and mobility, especially considering 3,500 new homes. D66 wants to combine this with attention to safe cycling routes and good public transport so Rhenen remains accessible for everyone.

122608.explanation: The plan for the city harbor has already been approved, including the surrounding area's approach. This offers sufficient facilities for residents and visitors. Additional expenses for a new marina are therefore unnecessary.

122609.explanation: D66 supports sustainable energy and wants to maintain control over the execution. Opposing ('kicking against the pricks') does not help; it is better to collaborate on wind projects that contribute to climate goals, with attention to impact on the surroundings and residents so that nature and the living environment are preserved.

122610.explanation: D66 wants to improve traffic safety and flow. A tunnel at the intersection of Achterbergsestraatweg reduces traffic pressure and makes routes safer for cars, cyclists, and pedestrians, as described in the election program.

122556.explanation: As CU, we stand for good participation through and with residents; therefore, a referendum is unnecessary.

122559.explanation: The municipality is responsible for managing and maintaining a good (physical) living environment for all residents, but there are limits to the possibilities.

122560.explanation: There are subsidies available from the national government for this. The municipality must support residents in applying for the government subsidies available.

122561.explanation: Space is scarce. We prefer to use it for businesses from which the municipality of Rhenen and its residents benefit more (for example, through employment).

122562.explanation: As CU, we have an eye for every person, including refugees. The number of refugees to be accommodated should be appropriate to the size of our municipality. This way, we contribute to a fair distribution in the Netherlands.

122563.explanation: Employers themselves are responsible for housing; this does not have to be obligatorily on their own business premises.

122566.explanation: As an alternative to the fireworks ban, the municipality can facilitate associations in organizing (or having organized) a fireworks show.

122567.explanation: This reduces the incentive for recycling and waste reduction.

122568.explanation: Clear rules have been established for this. However, it is important to regularly evaluate the policy.

122574.explanation: The municipality can think along and facilitate where necessary when sports associations are struggling.

122578.explanation: As CU, we encourage building and strengthening social contacts in neighborhoods so that more camera surveillance is not needed.

122579.explanation: This facility already exists in Rhenen.

122583.explanation: Holiday homes are often located in vulnerable natural areas; the environment is not equipped for certain facilities (such as access for emergency services); homes often do not meet requirements for permanent residences.

122585.explanation: When this is appropriate in combination with the personal circumstances of residents.

122592.explanation: The selection of organizations is done in consultation with the region. Quality is the most important focus; price is also considered.

122607.explanation: As CU, we aim for dialogue with the province to accelerate addressing traffic bottlenecks, including the Rhine bridge. We stand for cooperation with parties-exerting pressure does not fit with that.

122556.explanation: CDA believes it is important that residents can have a say. A referendum does not always help and does not suit everyone well. We prefer early and open discussions with residents to come to good choices together.

122559.explanation: Farmers are important to our municipality. We want to give them the space to operate. That is why we are positive about shops or campsites at farming businesses, as long as this fits well with the environment.

122560.explanation: CDA appreciates when residents jointly generate sustainable energy. We are happy to think along and help with rules and space. We are more reserved regarding financial support.

122561.explanation: CDA wants to keep our communities livable and quiet. Large distribution centers do not fit well. Therefore, we are very cautious about allowing distribution centers so that traffic and nuisance for residents remain limited.

122562.explanation: CDA finds it important that people needing shelter are well supported. A large asylum seekers’ center does not fit our municipality. Shelter must be appropriate so it remains bearable for residents and well arranged for newcomers.

122563.explanation: Seasonal workers deserve decent and safe housing. Employers must take that responsibility and arrange housing.

122566.explanation: A central fireworks show brings our community together and strengthens the sense of connection that is central to our program. It is a safe and festive way to celebrate the new year.

122567.explanation: We understand that free bulky waste collection sounds pleasant, but as CDA we believe municipal money must be spent responsibly. Free collection leads to higher costs for all residents. We choose a fair distribution of costs and prefer to invest in facilities.

122568.explanation: We want festivals and parties to remain enjoyable for everyone. Noise standards protect livability in our communities and ensure peace, health, and safety. This way, we find the balance between space for fun and respect for neighbors, fitting a connected and caring community.

122571.explanation: We find sports important. The CDA wants to lower barriers and ensure everyone can participate, but we do not believe municipalities should be obliged to pay for sports memberships. There are other ways to achieve solidarity.

122573.explanation: We find music lessons for children important. But we do not want the municipality to subsidize all music lessons. We choose to direct money first to basic facilities so that aid reaches those who need it most.

122574.explanation: We see how important sports clubs are for socializing and health. But we believe support must be tailored. The municipality helps where really needed but does not automatically contribute financially to every club.

122578.explanation: We believe this should only be done where really necessary. Together with residents and police, we choose targeted deployment so that Rhenen remains safe without unnecessary cameras.

122579.explanation: It is important that there is a safe place for people who are no longer safe at home. But a women's shelter does not fit the scale of Rhenen. We opt for quick, good shelter in the region and ensure residents can get safe help here.

122583.explanation: Recreational parks have their own purpose: space for peace, nature, and tourism. We want to preserve that. Therefore, we believe vacation homes should not become permanent residences. Living belongs in our residential centers, where we deliberately build suitable and affordable homes for the inhabitants of Rhenen.

122585.explanation: Everyone should contribute according to their ability, but always in a way that fits their situation. That's why we focus on social engagement that supports, not obliges or punishes.

122587.explanation: We remain neutral about raising the income limit. First, we will investigate what is needed in Rhenen and focus on tailor-made solutions to keep support fair and affordable. Income policy is not a local responsibility.

122591.explanation: Everyone deserves care that suits who they are. The CDA believes you should feel safe and respected. Therefore, you must always be able to choose a caregiver who aligns with your faith, culture, or conviction, without exclusion.

122592.explanation: We understand that costs play a role, but children deserve the best help. In the CDA program, quality and appropriate care come first, not just the price. It is about what works for the child and the family, with care that is reliable and nearby.

122593.explanation: We would like to lower the property tax (OZB) so that residents pay less tax. At the same time, we must be realistic: prices are rising and the municipality must continue its work. That is why we seek a fair balance between reducing burdens and having enough money for facilities.

122594.explanation: Rhenen uses this to pay for dog facilities and tackling nuisance. The CDA wants careful choices: abolishing fees can only be done if facilities and livability are not jeopardized.

122597.explanation: More shopping Sundays may seem attractive, but the CDA wants to protect the livability of our centers and our small entrepreneurs. Extra shopping Sundays can actually increase pressure on our small shops, which find it difficult to keep up with longer opening hours.

122599.explanation: Agricultural land is valuable for food and nature. That is why we do not want to sacrifice it for large-scale solar fields. Due to geopolitical developments, we are becoming increasingly dependent on local food supply.

122601.explanation: The CDA wants to keep our neighborhoods safe and livable. We want to firmly tackle drug use and nuisance. That is why a coffeeshop does not fit with what we want for Rhenen: we choose safety, peace, and healthy streets.

122603.explanation: Appropriate high-rise buildings help to create more homes without sacrificing the green outskirts. This way, Rhenen remains compact, livable, and future-proof, with more space for starters and seniors close to facilities.

122605.explanation: The CDA understands that parking in the center can sometimes be difficult. We want to keep the inner city accessible for families and small entrepreneurs, but balanced with livability. More parking spots are possible, as long as the street remains pleasant and safe.

122607.explanation: Widening the Rhine bridge seems like a solution, but without addressing the rest of the N233 and the intersection at Achterberg, traffic will still get stuck. Extra pressure makes Rhenen less livable, splits the city, and attracts more cut-through traffic from the A12 and A15.

122608.explanation: We are in favor of a marina, but not if it is paid for with your taxes. The CDA wants investments to truly add value for residents and be financially responsible. A marina can be realized, but with other financing.

122609.explanation: We are against wind turbines in the Binnenveld. They cause health complaints for residents and damage the open landscape. The CDA wants to protect this area so that nature and living environment remain preserved for current and future generations.

122610.explanation: A tunnel at the Achterbergsestraatweg increases traffic safety, improves flow, and protects livability. It is a future-oriented investment that fits the CDA values of safety, accessibility, and stewardship.

122556.explanation: Of course, we are in favor of more direct democracy on important issues.

122561.explanation: It does not really seem to be an issue in Rhenen, but we still have some space at Remmerden.

122562.explanation: Asylum seeker center, GET RID OF IT!

122563.explanation: We can at least initially strive for that.

122566.explanation: Not a municipal task, but of course a permit should be granted to an association if they want it.

122567.explanation: Take it to the waste station or, if possible, put it on Marktplaats. Otherwise, indeed, have it picked up for a fee.

122568.explanation: That is not necessary in Rhenen!

122571.explanation: Not automatically and always; fight poverty in other ways.

122573.explanation: Also not a primary task of the municipality.

122574.explanation: Also not a primary task of the municipality.

122578.explanation: Actually not necessary either, but to a limited extent it might add something in the center.

122579.explanation: Also not a primary task of the municipality.

122583.explanation: Preferably not, but due to the mismanagement of the Rutte cabinets, unfortunately it is now necessary.

122585.explanation: Unless it is medically really impossible.

122587.explanation: Only for the truly poor; otherwise fight poverty in a better way, for example by helping people to find work or manage their finances better.

122591.explanation: Complete nonsense! That would not make any sense at all... Here too, only the qualities of the caregiver matter. Caregivers on duty are like civil servants on duty, wearing uniforms.

122592.explanation: Yes, the national government has given municipalities a way too limited budget, so we have to watch the price.

122593.explanation: Definitely! Being frugal with tax revenues is indeed a primary task of every government, and away with left-wing hobbies! :-D

122594.explanation: There is no cat or pigeon tax either! But cleaning up dog poop should be mandatory.

122597.explanation: Same, it is in the interest of entrepreneurs and therefore of the municipality.

122599.explanation: We will not give up fertile agricultural land for that: first install on roofs.

122601.explanation: We are in favor of full but strict legalization of soft drugs.

122603.explanation: That does not fit with us at all!

122605.explanation: Good for retail, and they should remain free; however, retail can contribute somewhat.

122607.explanation: Yes of course, finally stop talking and start building, the provinces should have started 8 years ago.

122608.explanation: Yes, promoting tourism, the directly involved entrepreneurs should (must) of course also contribute.

122609.explanation: Wind turbines: NO! We are opposed to onshore wind turbines in any case.

122610.explanation: That is a provincial road, so it is a task of the province.

122556.explanation: It is good if residents can truly decide!

122559.explanation: As long as it does not harm local residents and the immediate surroundings.

122560.explanation: It should not be the case that the rest of the community has to bear the consequences of choices made by a few.

122561.explanation: We all order things online, so distribution centers are necessary; the best is to build them along major infrastructure.

122562.explanation: We are against an asylum seekers center here; there is no support for it among residents. Also, the consequences of a continuous influx can no longer be absorbed in our society (housing shortage and facilities).

122563.explanation: It is important that the responsibility for cheap labor lies with the companies that hire them, so they do not compete on the housing market.

122566.explanation: We regret that ornamental fireworks are banned for private individuals. But assuming fireworks will be banned, we do want to honor the tradition during the New Year's Eve.

122567.explanation: This keeps the streets and surroundings clean and prevents illegal dumping.

122571.explanation: It is important that everyone has access to sports, but that receiving benefits should not automatically lead to responsibility falling on the municipality.

122578.explanation: False security that violates the privacy of ordinary people and does not contribute to crime enforcement.

122579.explanation: There must be a tougher approach to perpetrators of domestic violence. We might as well build a detention complex for the perpetrators under the town hall.

122583.explanation: As long as there is a significant housing shortage, it would be good to temporarily allow vacation homes as permanent residences. Naturally, this should be balanced with the surroundings.

122585.explanation: It is important that everyone has the opportunity to participate in society.

122587.explanation: It is important that we support people to move forward and do not accept systematic poverty as a fact.

122592.explanation: Both quality and price ratio are important.

122593.explanation: We want to freeze the property tax (OZB) for the next four years! This forces the municipality to make better choices about their expenditures.

122594.explanation: It is an unjust tax that does not contribute to the problems related to dogs.

122605.explanation: It is important for entrepreneurs and residents that sufficient parking spaces are available.

122610.explanation: It is important for safety and the connection between Achterberg and Rhenen.

statements.title: Rhenen should exert more pressure to have the Rhine Bridge widened quickly.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The residents of Rhenen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Rhenen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Rhenen should install more camera surveillance (CCTV).

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Rhenen should establish a women's shelter (domestic violence shelter).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
High-rise residential buildings may be permitted in Rhenen.
Rhenen should spend more money on building a new marina.
Rhenen should invest in a tunnel at the intersection of Achterbergsestraatweg.
Rhenen should oppose the installation of wind turbines in the Binnenveld.

titleParts.text: Rhenen moet zich verzetten tegen de komst van

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
